food_subgroup: Western breakfast platter with eggs, poultry sausage, poultry ham, bread, vegetables, and salad

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a composite Western breakfast platter. FOODON_00002473 (breakfast food) is the closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (egg-based mixed dishes) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for a composite Western breakfast platter with sausage, ham, bread, and salad.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept maps to a composite Western breakfast platter at this specificity level.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cafe breakfast plate comprising scrambled eggs (~80 g), chicken sausage (~60 g), chicken ham (~40 g), garlic baguette (~60 g), sautéed tomatoes and mushrooms (~80 g), and a side salad (~130 g), totalling approximately 450 g.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — composite estimation from SR Legacy entries: Egg, whole, cooked scrambled (NDB 01132); Chicken sausage, cooked (NDB 05337); Chicken, roasted, meat only (used as ham proxy) (NDB 05013); French bread/baguette (NDB 18033); Tomatoes, cooked (NDB 11530); Mushrooms, cooked (NDB 11260); Lettuce/salad greens, raw (NDB 11252)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA or HPB entry exists for this composite Western breakfast platter. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted-average composition of individual USDA SR Legacy components proportional to serving weights: scrambled eggs 80 g, chicken sausage 60 g, chicken ham 40 g, garlic baguette 60 g, sautéed tomatoes 40 g, sautéed mushrooms 40 g, salad greens 130 g. Garlic butter on baguette estimated at ~8 g added fat. Values are per 100 g of the composite platter.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimates for mixed Western breakfast components (scrambled eggs GI ~0, white bread/baguette GI ~75, chicken sausage GI ~28, vegetables GI ~15)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this composite breakfast platter. GI estimated as a carbohydrate-weighted average of components: baguette/bread (GI 75, ~27 g carb contribution), scrambled eggs (GI ~0, negligible carb), chicken sausage (GI ~28, ~2 g carb), vegetables and salad (GI ~15, ~4 g carb combined), garlic butter (negligible carb). Weighted composite GI ≈ 52 (low-medium boundary, classified low). Glycemic load per 450 g serving = (52 × 46.8 g carbohydrate) / 100 = 24.3, rounded to 24.4. Sources: Atkinson et al. 2008 AJCN; University of Sydney GI Database.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~420 mg/100 g; ~1890 mg per full serving) from chicken sausage, chicken ham, and garlic baguette
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this meal as a single serving provides approximately 82% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg, primarily from processed poultry meats and salted bread; requesting reduced-sodium sausage and ham or halving the processed meat portion is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~24) driven by the garlic baguette component; protein and fat from eggs and poultry slow postprandial glucose rise
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be aware that the garlic baguette contributes the majority of the glycemic load (~24 per serving); substituting a wholegrain or lower-GI bread option and pairing with the high-protein egg and poultry components can help moderate postprandial blood glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Cholesterol (~145 mg/100 g; ~652 mg per serving) from scrambled eggs, and saturated fat from sausage and garlic butter
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should note that this meal is high in dietary cholesterol from eggs and contains saturated fat from chicken sausage and garlic butter; limiting to occasional consumption, choosing leaner poultry products, and reducing garlic butter quantity can help manage cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Breakfast Special is a Western-style cafe breakfast platter served at The Tree Cafe (Funan, Singapore) and similar Singaporean Western cafes. It consists of scrambled eggs, chicken sausage, chicken ham, a garlic baguette, sautéed tomatoes and mushrooms, and a fresh side salad. The dish is a Singaporean adaptation of the classic British or American full breakfast, substituting pork products with halal-certified chicken alternatives, making it widely accessible to Muslim diners. The scrambled eggs are soft and creamy, the chicken sausage is lightly seasoned and pan-grilled, the chicken ham is thinly sliced and mildly smoky, and the garlic baguette is toasted with garlic butter for a crisp exterior. The sautéed tomatoes and mushrooms add umami depth and acidity, while the salad provides freshness and textural contrast. Nutritionally, the meal is a moderate-energy, high-protein breakfast (~148 kcal/100 g; ~666 kcal per 450 g serving) with meaningful contributions of B vitamins, iron, and vitamin A from the egg and poultry components. The primary clinical concerns are high sodium from processed poultry meats and the moderate glycemic load from the baguette. It is suitable as an occasional full breakfast for healthy adults but requires modification for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease.
common_user_queries: What is in a Western breakfast at a Singapore cafe?
Calories in a full breakfast with eggs and chicken sausage
Nutritional value of scrambled eggs with chicken ham and baguette
Is this breakfast suitable for diabetics?
Chicken sausage and egg breakfast calories

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this composite Western breakfast platter as served at The Tree Cafe. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted-average composition of individual USDA SR Legacy component entries. GI is estimated from carbohydrate-weighted analogous values from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008. Confidence is medium rather than low because all component sources are primary approved sources (USDA SR Legacy) and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Food name normalised from 'Breakfast Special ⭐' to 'Breakfast Special' (emoji removed for canonical naming). 2. Input food_group 'Desserts' and cuisine 'Coffee' appear to be GrabFood platform categorisation artefacts; corrected to food_group 'Mixed' and cuisine 'Western' based on dish description. 3. Serving size estimated at 450 g based on typical Singapore cafe Western breakfast platter weights. 4. Halal flag set to true as chicken sausage and chicken ham are standard halal substitutes in Singapore Western cafes; this should be verified against the specific restaurant's halal certification. 5. Nutrient values are per-100 g estimates derived from weighted-average USDA SR Legacy component data; actual restaurant values may differ. 6. Sodium is notably high (~420 mg/100 g) due to processed chicken sausage and ham; this is a key clinical concern. 7. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, and all B vitamins are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data and carry moderate uncertainty. 8. GI of 52 is a carbohydrate-weighted composite estimate; no direct GI measurement exists for this dish. 9. FoodOn FOODON_00002473 and FoodEx2 A0CKP are closest-parent matches only; no exact ontology code exists for this composite platter. 10. Price preserved from input: SGD 15.00 (single price point). 11. Restaurant: The Tree Cafe - Funan (1 restaurant on GrabFood Singapore as of data collection).
